title: Request Context, Deadlines, Cancellation, and Cleanup · Go Semantics Case: Goroutine Result Channel Ownership
summary: A request starts database and outbound work with background contexts, ignores disconnects, exhausts its entire deadline before cleanup, and leaks goroutines after middleware returns. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo goroutine result channel ownership increment in deadline budget and cancellation propagation trace. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Give every request goroutine a stop path, bounded result channel, single closer, retained error, and join or deliberate ownership transfer.
Distinguish client cancellation, server deadline, dependency timeout, shutdown, overload, and internal failure for response and observability decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Goroutine Result Channel Ownership · Theory Goroutine Result Channel Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2803 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive a dependency budget while reserving time for cancellation cleanup; then change the tenant and show deny-by-default before repository access. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Goroutine Result Channel Ownership contract for the supplied data.
